key_takeaways: GW32 Wildcard + GW33 Bench Boost + GW34 Free Hit + GW36 Triple Captain is the optimal full-chip strategy, especially for those with many chips left.
Those with strong teams in GW32 and lots of transfers can consider delaying the wildcard (e.g., Free Hit 33, Wildcard 35 strategy).
Bench Boost 33 has explosive potential but may not fit all teams.
Key teams to target for doubles/blanks: Chelsea, Brighton, Man City, Burnley, Leeds, Bournemouth (33 double, 34 blank), and Crystal Palace/Man City (possible 36 double).
Player picks should be tailored to each manager's chip status and squad composition; players with additional fixtures and consistent output are preferred.
reasoning: Squad structure and chip count dictate optimal timing—Wildcard 32 best maximizes doubles/bench boost.
GW33 Bench Boost offers most fixtures and upside.
Bruno and Haaland both have excellent captaincy claims thanks to form, fixtures, and reliability.
Avoiding rotation doubts and card risks increases security for key GWs.
Transferring out players with unreliable minutes (e.g., Ekitiké) is preferable unless transfer-limited.

key_takeaways: Wildcard use is almost evenly split—roughly half are going now, half later.

The optimal strategy varies depending on your existing team.
Arsenal players have dropped out of favor for doubles, which affects wildcard picks.
